goal: Add a side-effect-free dispatch/frontend readiness projection that combines the WLP contract draft, card-conditioned dispatch plan, and pre-registered claims gate into one deterministic status object for later runtime and cockpit adoption. Do not add a runtime route, worker dispatch, provider call, package dependency, live write, or frontend screen.
scope_out: Do not integrate readiness into orchestrateTask, server routes, existing claim gate, smart push, provider calls, or live dispatch.
Do not add or change frontend files.
Do not add package scripts, dependencies, feature flags, environment variables, or deployment instructions.
Do not change the semantics of BPK-003 through BPK-006 artifacts.
claims: Dispatch readiness can be derived deterministically from the WLP contract draft, card-conditioned dispatch plan, and pre-registered claims gate.
Dispatch is allowed only when card-conditioned dispatch and pre-registered claims both allow and the contract has explicit evidence requirements.
A frontend/cockpit projection can be produced without opening runtime dispatch or adding a UI.
The readiness projection is side-effect-free and tested independently.
assumptions: BPK-003 through BPK-006 are committed and available as pure pre-dispatch governance artifacts.
Frontend adoption should consume a projection contract first, not a live runtime route.
Runtime dispatch integration belongs to a later main block after this readiness projection is stable.
dod: builder/src/dispatchFrontendReadiness.ts exports side-effect-free readiness types and a projection function.
The projection returns dispatch_ready, frontend_review, or blocked with deterministic reasons.
The projection keeps frontend rendering separate from dispatch execution.
builder/tests/dispatchFrontendReadiness.test.ts covers ready, review, blocked, evidence-missing, task-mismatch, and no-mutation behavior.
review-packets/BPK-007.md records scope, safety invariants, gates, and verification.
The focused readiness test and npm run typecheck in builder/ pass.
stop_conditions: A runtime route, provider call, GitHub write, database write, deployment change, or frontend file becomes necessary.
Changing BPK-003, BPK-004, BPK-005, or BPK-006 semantics becomes necessary.
Package or dependency changes become necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-003 through BPK-006 created pure pre-dispatch governance artifacts and a generated command/schema manifest. BPK-007 should close the requested line with a pure readiness projection for later dispatch and frontend adoption.
